mission: Help local authorities reduce illegal dumping and adjacent public-space disruption by combining rapidly deployable field devices, compliant evidence workflows, and municipal operations software into one measurable enforcement system.

businessModel: Recurring municipal subscriptions for software, compliance support, and analytics
Device deployment, relocation, and fleet service fees
Multi-site expansion within councils and intermunicipal authorities
Premium compliance, onboarding, and legal-procedure support
Adjacent expansion from waste enforcement into neighborhood safety workflows
marketForces: Pressure on councils to show visible action against fly-tipping and anti-social behaviour
Strict GDPR, evidentiary, and public-space surveillance constraints
Shortage of field enforcement capacity and rising administrative burden
Demand for deployments that avoid roadworks, fiber rollout, and long procurement lead times
Need to prove public value through incident reduction, fines recovery, and resident confidence
keyPartners: Data protection officers and legal teams
Intermunicipal authorities and regional service operators

deliveryPrinciples: Start with concrete nuisance hotspots where councils can show visible resident impact quickly.
Keep deployment light enough to move with municipal reality rather than depend on heavy civil works.
Make evidence and case procedures admissible by default so field teams do not reconstruct legality manually.
Treat elected officials, police, and technical services as one coordinated operating system, not isolated users.
Prove value through fewer incidents, faster action, and lower administrative burden before broad expansion.

deliveryFlows.name: Hotspot deployment flow
deliveryFlows.description: A council identifies a recurring nuisance hotspot, validates privacy constraints, deploys a mobile device, and begins remote detection and evidence capture.

deliveryFlows.name: Incident-to-case flow
deliveryFlows.description: A suspicious event is qualified, evidence is secured, a compliant procedure pack is generated, and the case is handed to the right authority for enforcement.

deliveryFlows.name: Cleanup and recurrence prevention flow
deliveryFlows.description: Field services receive prioritized tasks, clear the site, and use recurrence analytics to decide whether equipment should remain, move, or escalate.

deliveryFlows.name: Executive accountability flow
deliveryFlows.description: Leaders track resident-visible outcomes, recovered fines, service productivity, and compliance posture across sites and municipalities.

organizationSizing.assumedCompanyContext: A municipal-tech company serving roughly 200 local authorities across multiple European markets with a blend of SaaS, connected devices, and compliance-heavy service operations.

groups.mission: Own siting, rollout, fleet health, connectivity, and reliability of the movable field device network.
Own admissible evidence workflows, GDPR controls, case preparation, and notice-generation foundations.
Own the operator console, mobile response flow, field coordination, and service integrations used day to day by councils.
Own executive reporting, deployment ROI proof, market expansion into safety, and repeatable adoption across municipalities.

operatingModelNotes: Municipal sales and product credibility depend on legally defensible workflows as much as on detection accuracy.
Customer success must stay close to deployment evidence because councils buy visible outcomes, not raw device counts.
The product should reduce municipal paperwork, not simply digitize it.
Safety expansion should reuse the same compliance, evidence, and executive-value foundations wherever possible.
